lines.greek: βῆ ῥʼ ἴμεν ἐκ θαλάμοιο, θύρην δʼ ἐπέρυσσε κορώνῃ
ἀργυρέῃ, ἐπὶ δὲ κληῖδʼ ἐτάνυσσεν ἱμάντι.
ἔνθʼ ὅ γε παννύχιος, κεκαλυμμένος οἰὸς ἀώτῳ,
βούλευε φρεσὶν ᾗσιν ὁδὸν τὴν πέφραδʼ Ἀθήνη.

interlinear: she-went then to-go from the-chamber, and-the-door she-pulled-to by-the-handle,
the-silver one, and-over-it the-bolt she-drew-tight with-the-thong.
there he, all-night-long, wrapped in a-fleece of-wool,
pondered in his-mind the-journey which had-shown Athena.
literal: Then she went out from the chamber, and pulled the door to by the handle,
the silver handle, and drew the bolt home with the thong.
There, all night long, wrapped in a fleece of wool,
he pondered in his mind the journey that Athena had shown him.
translations.elizabethan:
Then out she went, and drew the door to close
by its silver handle, and she shot the bolt
home with the leathern thong. And there all night,
wrapped in a fleece of wool, he lay and turned
within his mind the journey that Athena
had shown to him.
translations.modernist:
Then she went out of the room. She pulled the door shut by its silver handle and drew the bolt home with the strap.

He lay there all night, wrapped in a fleece of wool, thinking about the journey Athena had shown him.
translations.storybook:
Then out she went, and pulled the door shut behind her by its silver handle, and slid the bolt across with the leather strap.

And there he lay, all night long, wrapped up warm in a woolly fleece, thinking and thinking about the journey that Athena had shown him.
